title: apargidium boreale
intro: Apargidium boreale, also known as Bering's sticky catchfly, is a flowering plant species in the family Caryophyllaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been used to treat wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Apargidium boreale include reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and soothing coughs, making it a popular remedy in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
